text.title: Critical-Issue Triage Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules that turn post-session triage from a gut call into a defensible decision in 30 minutes.

rule.statement: Each finding MUST be assigned a severity 0-4 using the rubric: 0=cosmetic, 1=minor (workaround obvious), 2=major (workaround exists but is painful), 3=catastrophic (user cannot complete the primary task), 4=catastrophic+accessibility/safety. Severity assignment is committed in the triage table with the one-line evidence ("user 3 quit after 7 attempts").

rationale.source: Nielsen severity rating scale + practitioner adaptations

rule.rationale: A consistent 0-4 scale across sessions allows trend analysis ("we have 4 sev-3 findings this quarter"). Ad-hoc severity language ("really bad", "kinda bad") makes triage incomparable.

rule.statement: Frequency MUST be expressed as a band against observed N: SINGLE (1/N), FEW (2-3/N or <= 30%), MANY (40-70% of N), MOST (> 70%). Raw counts without a band are rejected — bands are what the action matrix consumes. "One user hit it" is not actionable; "1/N with severity 4" is.

rationale.source: Sample-size heuristic from Jakob Nielsen, '5 users find 85% of usability problems'

rule.rationale: With small N (5-8), a single occurrence may already represent a real population issue. Bands acknowledge sample limits without dismissing one-off severe findings.

rule.statement: Context modifiers MUST be applied: REVENUE-CRITICAL path (checkout, signup) bumps action by one level; REGULATED user (medical, financial, accessibility-rights) bumps by one level; INTERNAL-TOOL path may demote by one level. The modifier is recorded so the rationale is auditable.

rationale.source: Risk-management practice; impact varies by path criticality

rule.rationale: A confusing button in admin settings is different from the same button on the checkout page. The modifier captures the business context the raw severity ignores.

rule.statement: Action MUST come from the matrix lookup: severity × frequency × context-modifier → SAME-DAY-FIX / NEXT-SPRINT / BACKLOG / DISCARD. The lookup is deterministic. Overrides are allowed but require a written one-line justification ("override to same-day because customer demo tomorrow"). Verbal overrides are rejected.
rule.rationale: Deterministic lookup prevents the recurring "loud finding" bias — a finding that was vivid in observation gets escalated past its real severity.

example.title: Triage row with rationale
example.code: Finding: User cannot find "Add to cart" button on mobile product page (button is below the fold and color contrast 2.5:1). Severity: 3 (catastrophic — primary purchase path blocked) Frequency: MANY (4/5 mobile users) Context: REVENUE-CRITICAL +1 -> effective severity 4 Action: SAME-DAY-FIX Owner: UI lead, eng to deploy by EOD Rationale: Sev-3 + MANY + REVENUE-CRITICAL hits matrix top-left.
example.note: Rubric applied, modifier recorded, owner named, action deterministic.

example.title: Improvised triage
example.code: "User 3 was really frustrated by the cart icon. Should we fix it ASAP?"
example.note: Single user, no severity, no frequency, no modifier, ambiguous action. Loud-user bias.
